transcript: Thanks again for joining Dan and Emily's pickling show today. Right now Emily is actually dumping salt into the 8 cups of water to create the fermenting juice that will make the cucumbers turn into solid pickles. Tune in. Hello again, we're going to be now cutting the cucumbers. I'll be cutting off the tips to make sure to do that. That way it brings and lets the juices flow a little better on this. I will cut the other side down, make sure you really pop that off. Now what I'm going to do, these are a little longer than the jar, so I'll be popping them in half and then i'm going to cut them in fours because we like our pickles and fours so that would be kind of fun right now what you'll want to do is make sure to go to the market store and get the pickle spice it's a variety of different beans inside there what we're going to do is going to put a pinch of this stuff kind of the bottom of each of them before we actually get it so the pickles be on top of it or should I say the cucumbers at this point have not formed into that great friend the pickle. Back here is the actual process of putting in the cucumbers into our jars. Now you don't want to tightly pack it. You want to make sure it kind of feels loose and nice and beautiful like that. You can see these are nice and good. So actually put in some peppers in one of them just mostly because I like the spice that comes with the peppers to bring into the juice there. I also put garlic clove in there as well. The flavors that the garlic is able to bring is absolutely stunning. Of course at that point we're gonna want to make sure we put some bay leaves in there as well as the dill weed to make sure that it actually brings in that beautiful flavor that we want to see. Here we are with our brine. Is that the correct word? Brine. Brine. We will now pour those into our jars. I want to make sure you go up to the top here and then of course with the actual jar itself we want to make sure we get a nice tight fit on there let's go ahead and tighten that up nice and tight and what you're gonna want to do is of course give this nice little shake up also you're just gonna want to put this away for about a week in a hidden place. Not in the refrigerator because the refrigerator actually stops the brining or the pickling process.
